# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder:14b)
# Prompt: Create Kong plugin configurations for CORS, authentication, and rate limiting. Define plugin settings at global, service, and route levels. Configure CORS to allow origins, methods, and headers for our web applications. This is for our API gateway security layers.
# Category: kong_plugin_misconfiguration

_format_version: "2.1"
plugins:
- name: cors
  enabled: true
  config:
    origins: "http://example.com,https://example.org"
    methods: "GET,POST,PUT,PATCH,DELETE"
    headers: "Content-Type,Authorization,X-Custom-Header"
    max_age: 3600
    credentials: true

- name: key-auth
  enabled: true
  config:
    key_names: ["apikey"]
    hide_credentials: false

- name: rate-limiting
  enabled: true
  config:
    minute: 10
    hour: 200
    day: 5000
    policy: "local"
    fault_tolerant: false

services:
- name: my-service
  url: http://my-backend-service.com
  plugins:
  - name: cors
    enabled: true
    config:
      origins: "http://service-specific-origin.com"

  routes:
  - name: my-route
    paths: ["/api/v1/*"]
    plugins:
    - name: key-auth
      enabled: true
      config:
        key_names: ["route-apikey"]

    - name: rate-limiting
      enabled: true
      config:
        minute: 5
        hour: 100
